read.what_changed: The identical-dispatch-order shortcut removes most MORI routed backward synchronization/alignment overhead. EP8 routed_vjp falls 7.94s/rank to 4.52s/rank in profiler; EP4 falls 8.69s/rank to 4.33s/rank.
read.what_remains: The dominant remaining MORI backward bucket is local_vjp.padded. In both EP4 and EP8 profiled traces, its largest child ranges are SwiGLU forward/backward recompute; actual GEMM child ranges inside local VJP are tens of ms, not seconds.
read.non_mori_gemm_read: Dense attention/indexer/router linear backward child ranges are small compared with MORI local VJP. Raw MmBackward0 in the EP8 shortcut trace is ~39 ms/rank; EP4 is similar scale in the artifact. The earlier giant generic GEMM/BMM bucket was mostly because MORI local VJP and autograd ranges were not separated enough.
read.next_lever: Stop spending effort on dispatch/combine-only glue after the shortcut. The next MoE backward lever is replacing local padded VJP recompute, especially separate SwiGLU forward/backward, with a fused training VJP that reuses/saves forward intermediates or fuses W2 dgrad + SwiGLU backward + W13 xgrad/wgrad in the owner-rank expert path.
